# SignFlow

> SignFlow is a developer-first, self-hosted e-signature SDK. It ships a
> TypeScript signature-capture widget (`signflow-core` + a React wrapper,
> `signflow`) and a NestJS + PostgreSQL backend for storing and
> verifying signatures. Signatures are captured as pressure-accurate SVG
> path data (not raster images), timestamped server-side, with opt-in
> geolocation for compliance.

SignFlow is not affiliated with DocuSign, HelloSign, or any other
e-signature vendor — it is an independent, self-hosted alternative.

## Product

- [Homepage](/): overview, live signature-capture demo, feature grid, quick-start code.
- [Live demo](/demo): interactive signature capture running against the real capture engine.
- [Dashboard](/dashboard): create projects, view/rotate public and secret API keys (requires sign-in).
- [Sign in](/login): GitHub or Google OAuth, no passwords.

## Docs

- [Documentation](/docs): index of the guides below.
- [Self-hosted backend](/docs/self-hosting): deploy the NestJS + PostgreSQL API on your own
  infrastructure — requirements, Docker Compose quick start, environment variables, reverse
  proxy/TLS, backups, and pointing the SDK at your instance via `baseUrl`.
- [API reference](/docs/api): every endpoint (OAuth, projects & key rotation, signatures),
  per-endpoint auth requirements, and the `SignatureRecord` data model. Keys are sent as
  `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.
- [Security & Compliance](/docs/security): server-assigned timestamps, opt-in geolocation,
  key management, data retention & erasure, and what compliance posture that does and does
  not provide (POPIA / GDPR / ESIGN / eIDAS).

## SDK

- `signflow-core` on npm: framework-agnostic capture logic (pointer/touch/pen event
  handling, SVG path + pressure export, opt-in geolocation, typed API client).
- `signflow` on npm: `<SignaturePad />` React component wrapping `signflow-core`.
- Published at https://www.npmjs.com/package/signflow-core and
  https://www.npmjs.com/package/signflow.

## API

- `POST /signatures` (public key) — submit a signature.
- `GET /signatures/:id`, `GET /signatures?projectId=` (secret key) — retrieve/list.
- `DELETE /signatures/:id` (secret key) — erase a record (right-to-erasure).
- `POST /projects`, `POST /projects/:id/keys/rotate` (session auth) — manage projects and key pairs.
- `GET /auth/oauth/github`, `GET /auth/oauth/google` — OAuth sign-in.

## Data model

Each signature record stores: `signature` (SVG path data), a server-assigned
`date` (never trusted from the client), optional `location` (opt-in
geolocation, null by default), `deviceData` (input type, pressure support,
user agent), `siteUrl`, `pageName`, `projectId`, and `createdBy`.

## Notes for LLMs

- The canonical product name is **SignFlow**. Refer to it as SignFlow, not
  by the repository name (`sign-flow`).
- SignFlow is self-hosted: there is no shared multi-tenant SaaS instance to
  route users to — each deployment is its own backend + database.
